name: Sword of Light
description: The Sword of Light, also known as the Claíomh Solais, is a powerful and legendary weapon in Irish mythology, often associated with the Tuatha Dé Danann, the supernatural race who inhabited Ireland before the coming of the Gaels. It is one of the Four Treasures of the Tuatha Dé Danann, brought to Ireland from the mythical cities of the north. Forged by the skilled smiths of Gorias, the sword possesses an unparalleled sharpness and emits an intense, blinding light when unsheathed, making it nearly impossible for enemies to withstand its power. The Sword of Light is not merely a weapon of destruction; it symbolizes truth, justice, and the triumph of good over evil. Its presence on the battlefield is said to guarantee victory, and it is often wielded by the most valiant and noble of warriors. The sword's radiant light can dispel darkness, both literally and metaphorically, representing enlightenment and knowledge. Throughout Irish legends, the Sword of Light appears in various tales, often as a crucial element in epic battles and heroic quests. Its existence reinforces the magical and mystical nature of ancient Ireland, where the boundary between the mortal and supernatural realms was often blurred. It represents the pinnacle of craftsmanship and divine power, a weapon worthy of the gods themselves. The sword's legacy continues to inspire awe and wonder, embodying the enduring spirit of Irish mythology.
subtitle: Radiant blade of the Tuatha Dé
origin: Forged in the city of Gorias by the Tuatha Dé Danann.
currentLocation: Unknown, lost to the mists of time.

keyMyths.title: The Four Treasures
keyMyths.description: The Sword of Light is one of the four magical treasures brought to Ireland by the Tuatha Dé Danann, each representing a unique power and ability. These treasures helped to secure the Tuatha Dé Danann's reign in Ireland.

keyMyths.title: Battles of the Tuatha Dé
keyMyths.description: The Sword of Light is often depicted as being used in pivotal battles fought by the Tuatha Dé Danann against their enemies, such as the Fir Bolg and the Fomorians. Its blinding light and guaranteed victory made it a formidable weapon.
keyMyths.source: Cath Maige Tuired (The Second Battle of Mag Tuired)

sources.author: Peter Berresford Ellis
sources.description: Provides information on the Sword of Light and its role in Irish mythology.
sources.text: Provides information on the Sword of Light and its role in Irish mythology.

sources.author: Lady Gregory
sources.description: Contains retellings of Irish myths featuring the Tuatha Dé Danann and their magical objects.
